title: Named volumes as Btrfs subvolumes?
body:
When using the Btrfs driver in Podman, containers are stored as individual subvolumes.
However, my most important data is stored in named volumes which are not stored in individual subvolumes.
It would be nice if named volumes were created as Btrfs subvolumes when using the Btrfs driver.
This should make it simpler for anyone who wants to take snapshots of named subvolumes.

I can also think of instances where users might want to use subvolumes for named volumes in order to modify particular mount options.
This might be desired for databases for example.
Changing mount options would require the subvolume be in a flat layout, but creating it as a subvolume makes it slightly easier for end users to achieve this.

This might be out of scope, but I wanted to put it out there since taking snapshots of this data would follow my backup workflow.

comments.body: This should really be under Podman not in storage. And I think this would be best handled via a volume plugin perhaps.

It would add significant complexity to the code - right now they’re just
folders, no involvement of the storage driver and c/storage at all.
Theoretically possible, but I don’t know if the benefits justify the added
complexity.

I think this should be possible right now with volume drivers, though? Just
need to get one written that uses btrfs as a backend. Or even the built in
volumes with Mount options. Just a question of how complex the command line
to invoke will be.

On Sat, Jun 12, 2021 at 04:59 Daniel J Walsh ***@***.***>
wrote:

A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I'm going to close this; this should be implemented by an external volume plugin, not in Podman itself.
